question.text: What is the lock-in period for minimum promoters' contribution in an IPO when majority of issue proceeds are for capital expenditure?

answer.text: 3 years from the date of allotment
18 months from the date of allotment
6 months from the date of allotment
1 year from the date of allotment

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When the majority of issue proceeds (excluding OFS portion) is for capital expenditure, the minimum promoters' contribution is locked-in for 3 years from allotment date. In normal cases, the lock-in is 18 months. **The Trap:** 18 months (option b) is the standard lock-in for minimum promoters' contribution when proceeds are not primarily for capex. The 3-year lock-in applies specifically for capex-heavy IPOs. **Cross-Exam Context:** Excess promoter holding (above minimum 20%) is locked for 6 months normally, or 1 year if majority proceeds are for capex. SEBI uses different lock-in periods to ensure promoter accountability. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Lock-in periods: Minimum contribution = 18 months (normal) / 3 years (capex). Excess holding = 6 months (normal) / 1 year (capex).
